discrepancies.title: Lean type-check parse-failure: Mathlib4 not on path
discrepancies.description: Bare `lean m5_frds.lean` invocation in scratch tmp dir failed with 'unknown module prefix Mathlib' at L55 (the `import Mathlib.Tactic` line). This is a Mathlib4 dependency-resolution failure, not a syntax error in the FRD body. Per relay spec D.P2, this is logged as non-blocking; the FRD scaffold proceeds without resolution. A subsequent fire (M10-PROOF-DRAFT) staged inside a Lake project with Mathlib4 on path will exercise full type-checking.

discrepancies.title: R(h) ratio is partial at aPoly h = 0
discrepancies.description: The B2 FRD `def R (V : VQuad) (h : ℚ) : ℚ := V.bPoly h / V.aPoly h` is total over rationals but mathematically has a singularity at zeros of `aPoly`. The Lean encoding does not surface this as a `Decidable` predicate or a `Subtype`-narrowed domain. Future M10-PROOF-DRAFT or B2-discharge fire should refactor to `Option ℚ` or a `Subtype` carrying the non-vanishing hypothesis.

discrepancies.title: Convention drift: SIARC F(2,4) leading-first vs PCF-1 v1.3 (alpha,beta,gamma,delta,epsilon)
discrepancies.description: Relay 098 specifies V_quad parametrization as 5-tuple (a2, a1, a0, b1, b0) with `a` of degree 2 and `b` of degree 1, anchored to repo memory `f1-base-d2d4-complete-2026-04-22.md` line 24 (`a_coeffs = (a2, a1, a0)` with `a(n) = a2 n^2 + a1 n + a0`). PCF-1 v1.3 (= p12_journal_main.tex § 1.1 lines 113-130) uses the inverse role convention `a_n = delta n + epsilon` (deg 1), `b_n = alpha n^2 + beta n + gamma` (deg 2). The two conventions enumerate the same d=2 PCF family up to a label swap a <-> b. The Lean scaffold follows the relay convention literally per relay 098 Phase B B1 spec; the PCF-1 v1.3 convention is documented in m5_frds.lean header comment.

discrepancies.title: Picture v1.19 SHA-16 drift vs relay-claimed anchor
discrepancies.description: Relay 098 Phase A.P1 stated 'picture_v1.19 SHA16 4D852C97DBAC750 (per stored memory PICTURE chain)'. Actual SHA-256 of sessions/2026-05-06/PICTURE-V19-CONSOLIDATED-DEPOSIT/picture_v1.19.md = 8BD9043370872F078F05DE99AC031A8AE78C321EC75F49102ABC01F549326DAB (SHA-16 prefix 8BD9043370872F07). The relay's stated anchor 4D852C97DBAC750 does not match. Two interpretations: (a) the relay-quoted SHA-16 is from an earlier picture_v1.18 or pre-deposit draft snapshot; (b) memory file `picture-v19-consolidated-deposit-2026-05-06.md` records a different anchor than the deposited file. Used the actual on-disk SHA-256 8BD9043370872F07... in the Lean header per defensive principle. Non-blocking.

discrepancies.title: B7 statement-stub uses `True` placeholder rather than substantive predicate
discrepancies.description: B7 `M8b_axis_residual_acceptance` is a statement-only stub with proposition `True` and proof `by trivial`. The substantive content (the M8b axis sub-leading Stokes constant S_2 PERMANENT_RESIDUAL_VIA_BOREL_PADE verdict from relay 092) is not encoded in the Lean type. The relay spec explicitly authorizes this: 'Lean idiom: theorem M8b_axis_residual_acceptance : ... (statement-only stub; proof = sorry)'. The `True` body is actually stronger than a sorry-stub (no sorries; the proposition is just trivial). Tracked in coverage_gap_analysis.md row B7 and mathlib4_anchor_opportunities.md row B7.
